channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-8929] sanity-lfsck test_5 test failed to respond and timed out
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for nasf <fan.yong@intel.com>

Please provide additional information about the failure here.

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/d5792f0e-be6d-11e6-8111-5254006e85c2.

The message on the MDS:

19:03:40:[13134.629408] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl lfsck_start -M lustre-MDT0000 -t namespace -r
19:03:40:[13134.772866] LDISKFS-fs error (device dm-0): __ldiskfs_new_inode:987: comm lfsck: failed to insert inode 25116: doubly allocated?
19:03:40:[13134.777288] Aborting journal on device dm-0-8.
19:03:40:[13134.779639] LDISKFS-fs (dm-0): Remounting filesystem read-only
19:03:40:[13134.781634] LDISKFS-fs error (device dm-0) in osd_trans_stop:1838: IO failure
19:03:40:[13134.783757] LustreError: 781:0:(osd_handler.c:1553:osd_trans_commit_cb()) transaction @0xffff880079a15400 commit error: 2
19:03:40:[13134.786214] LustreError: 1615:0:(osd_handler.c:1841:osd_trans_stop()) lustre-MDT0000: failed to stop transaction: rc = -5
19:03:40:[13134.917487] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n 		mdd.lustre-MDT0000.lfsck_namespace |
19:03:40:[13134.917487] 		awk '/^flags/ { print $2 }'
19:03:40:[13135.049251] LustreError: 1691:0:(lfsck_namespace.c:4371:lfsck_namespace_dump()) ASSERTION( lfsck->li_di_oit != ((void *)0) ) failed: 
19:03:40:[13135.053471] LustreError: 1691:0:(lfsck_namespace.c:4371:lfsck_namespace_dump()) LBUG
19:03:40:[13135.055733] Pid: 1691, comm: lctl
19:03:40:[13135.057650] 
19:03:40:[13135.057650] Call Trace:
19:03:40:[13135.061147]  [<ffffffffa072f7d3>] libcfs_debug_dumpstack+0x53/0x80 [libcfs]
19:03:40:[13135.063400]  [<ffffffffa072f841>] lbug_with_loc+0x41/0xb0 [libcfs]
19:03:40:[13135.065551]  [<ffffffffa0da95dc>] lfsck_namespace_dump+0x57c/0x580 [lfsck]
19:03:40:[13135.067689]  [<ffffffffa0d97c3f>] lfsck_dump+0xef/0x1f0 [lfsck]
19:03:40:[13135.069768]  [<ffffffffa0fb3020>] mdd_lfsck_namespace_seq_show+0x20/0x60 [mdd]
19:03:40:[13135.071866]  [<ffffffff812223ea>] seq_read+0xfa/0x3a0
19:03:40:[13135.073833]  [<ffffffff8126b61d>] proc_reg_read+0x3d/0x80
19:03:40:[13135.075749]  [<ffffffff811fdf9e>] vfs_read+0x9e/0x170
19:03:40:[13135.077654]  [<ffffffff811feb6f>] SyS_read+0x7f/0xe0
19:03:40:[13135.079528]  [<ffffffff81696709>] system_call_fastpath+0x16/0x1b
item.summary: sanity-lfsck test_5 test failed to respond and timed out

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
Oleg Drokin (oleg.drokin@intel.com) merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/24869/
Subject: LU-8929 lfsck: dumper gets current position properly
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: cffd726304667ea3d84b4b3c1a9b66565e5a5566

Fan Yong (fan.yong@intel.com) uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/24869
Subject: LU-8929 lfsck: dumper gets current position properly
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 13148533c54f38526e40e650afc436452bc1976d
